uncovered_specs.text: The admin logo in the upper left MUST use primary-logo-no-wordmark.svg
The wordmark 'Customer Experience' MUST appear to the right of the logo
Dark Mode MUST be the default mode
Dark Mode MUST use a palette of greys rather than blue/purple
The main page display areas MUST have a thinner border in Dark Mode
The sticky navbar at the top of the page MUST be medium-light grey in Light Mode
The conversation filter control background MUST NOT be white in Dark Mode
The conversation history background MUST NOT be light pink
Integration logos for Zendesk, Google Analytics, Mailchimp, Stripe and Shopify MUST have a height of 150px
The tier label 'professional' MUST be capitalized as 'Professional'
The activation control MUST have three dispositions: Red/'Deactivate' (disable widget), Green/'Activate' (ready to activate), Yellow/'Activate' (blocked by missing mandatory config)
When the system is initialized and some mandatory inputs are missing, the activation control MUST show Yellow/'Activate' disposition
The Deactivate action MUST go through a confirmation dialog
All current logo images MUST be replaced with the updated files from branding/logo/SVG/ and branding/logo/PNG/
The active selection text color in the sidebar MUST be white, not red
The chat history background color MUST be #19191A
If the taupe border (#938a87) cannot be 0.2px, the border color MUST be changed to #363636
Conversation volume chart MUST show data only from the actual deployment date, not synthetic historical data
Inbox search MUST return results matching the search term (e.g., searching 'product' must find messages containing 'product')

Each dashboard display area MUST have a tooltip/link to relevant documentation (Total conversations, Avg response time, Resolution rate, Customer satisfaction, Escalation rate, Daily conversation volume, Recent conversations, Top intents)
Each Billing & usage display area MUST have a tooltip/link to documentation (Current plan, Conversations used, Pack balance, Current overage, Estimated overage cost, Daily usage)
Conversation volume chart duration MUST match the selected timeframe (7d/14d/30d/90d) irrespective of data availability
Conversation volume chart MUST NOT display historical data from before the environment deployment date
The 'Discard' control MUST be functional and must work correctly
The error banner close ('X') control MUST be functional
Category and Status filters on the KB page MUST return results that match the filter criteria
The KB 'Freshness' value MUST NOT display '--' (must have a clear meaning)
The 'Cross-session learning' toggle MUST be greyed out or cause save to fail for tiers below Professional+
All admin UI formatters shall apply null-safety checks using optional chaining or nullish coalescing (e.g., Object.entries(field ?? {})).
Zapier shall be the visual styling reference for admin UI design patterns.
The 'Intents' section shall be renamed to use accessible, non-technical language (e.g., 'Topic Breakdown').
Inbox search shall search conversation content (message text), not just metadata fields.
Integration name text shall be displayed at 22px font size.
Unimplemented integrations shall display 'Coming Soon' labels instead of appearing as functional.
All non-proper-noun UI labels shall use sentence case (e.g., 'Brand voice' not 'Brand Voice').
German, Portuguese, Japanese, Chinese, and Korean shall be removed from the language selector UI. Spanish and French shall be displayed but greyed-out (disabled, not operable).
Discard action shall trigger a configuration re-fetch in all child pages so form fields display the current active values (not empty placeholders).
Integration card logos MUST use a 180x180 pixel square container
The sidebar logo MUST have separate dark mode and light mode variants
The light mode sidebar logo MUST use NEW-BLOCK-LOGO-HORIZONTAL-LIGHT.svg
The dark mode sidebar logo MUST use NEW-BLOCK-LOGO-HORIZONTAL-DARK.svg
Dark mode page background color MUST be #141414
Dark mode card/surface background color MUST be #1f1f1f
Dark mode card border color MUST be #272727
Table row borders MUST use the standard box border color (#272727)
Input and selection field borders MUST use the standard box border color (#272727)
Section heading underlines MUST use the standard box border color (#272727)
Accordion and expander borders MUST use the standard box border color (#272727)
Inbox pane backgrounds MUST use the sidebar background color (#0a0a0a)
Search field borders MUST use the standard box border color (#272727)
RAG lookup tag borders MUST use the standard box border color (#272727)
Chat bubble grey background MUST use the sidebar active-page highlight color
Highlighted/selected conversation row background MUST use the sidebar active-page highlight color
The sidebar logo MUST render in its natural colors with no CSS filter applied
Dark mode color hierarchy MUST follow: chrome #0a0a0a (darkest) -> page #141414 -> surface #1f1f1f -> border #272727 (lightest dark element)
Grey buttons in the admin UI MUST use the standard box border color (#272727) for their border
The SPA console (Service Provider Administrator console) MUST provide a graphical UI for observing traffic, monitoring consumption, tracking issues, and observing budget versus spend.
The 'Preview' panel MUST be removed from the Configuration page.
Documentation links that are broken or return 404 MUST be treated as a significant concern requiring immediate remediation.
A color palette worksheet MUST be created for the graphic designer showing all UI element types with their current hex colors in both Dark/Light mode, with input fields for new values.

The Setup wizard dismissal MUST be associated with a specific session, so when an administrator logs in with a new session and the system has never been in an active state, the Setup wizard MUST be triggered
A master list of all required tooltips and their documentation URLs MUST be maintained and addressed as a single work item
The Integrations partner logos MUST NOT have added vertical padding/margin, the dark grey boxes MUST be horizontally-oriented rectangles (not vertically-oriented), logo height MUST be 260px
Integration text and buttons grouped with a logo MUST be positioned to the right of the logo, with nothing below the logo

The Integrations page MUST NOT have a large dark-grey box containing multiple lighter grey boxes — the page body MUST be standard dark grey with lighter-grey boxes arranged in a column centered in the display area (remove one layer of box nesting)
Integration logo containers MUST use 180px x 180px dimensions with border-radius 8px, dark background (#141414), and 1px solid border (#272727)
The feature "Cross-session learning" on the pricing/features page MUST have a tooltip
The feature "Dedicated model training" on the pricing/features page MUST have a tooltip
The entitlement tier badge MUST display with proper capitalization (e.g., "Professional" not "professional")

The grouped configuration items box MUST include a control displaying the state of those grouped items and allowing 'Activate' changes, 'Roll-back' changes, or 'Discard' changes to restore all inputs to their currently Active state.
A tooltip MUST be added to the 'Professional' plan-type indicator in the sticky top navbar explaining what the plan means and what subscription plans are available.
All empty but essential inputs MUST be marked with the traditional red asterisk indicating that an appropriate input is required.
Brand primary color MUST be #ff3621 (orange-red)
Typography MUST use Inter for body text and JetBrains Mono for code (Option A)
The header top-right logo MUST use primary-logo-no-wordmark.svg

The sidebar footer MUST display 'Agent Red Customer Experience' above the version number, and below it: 'Brought to you by remakerdigital.com. Copyright (c) 2026 Remaker Digital, a DBA of VanDusen & Palmeter, LLC. All rights reserved.'
The wizard 'Activate now' MUST properly handle array values (e.g., escalation_keywords) by keeping them as arrays, not joining into comma-separated strings
The Add article dialog MUST allow upload of documents (not just text entry)
Sentence case MUST be used for all UI labels throughout the admin interface
The activation dialog field tags MUST accurately reflect actual configuration field states — no inaccurate labels for fields like google_analytics_enabled or fine_tuning_enabled
Refactoring R2 (response model consolidation) shall be implemented at P1 priority.
Refactoring R3 (middleware chain simplification) shall be deferred to post-launch.
Refactoring R5 (Cosmos client singleton) shall be rejected — current pattern is acceptable.
Refactoring R6 (error handler unification) shall be implemented at P2 priority.
All tier-gated features shall enforce backend tier gates — UI disabling alone is insufficient.
NATS topics shall be tenant-scoped using the pattern {tenant_id}.{agent} for conversation pipeline isolation.
PII scrubbing shall be applied at the logging layer — no customer-identifiable data in Application Insights telemetry.
Channel-specific grace periods shall be enforced: 48hr for Shopify (mandatory per Shopify requirements), 30-day for Stripe (per SLA).
DataExportService and DataDeletionService shall be implemented for GDPR data subject request handling.
Consent management shall be implemented for Persistent Customer Memory — consent gates PCM Layers 2-4, denial triggers L2-4 data deletion.
Noisy neighbor prevention shall use Option B+ (the selected supervisor recovery approach addressing unsupervised session risk).
The chat API module shall implement 6 endpoints: POST /conversations, POST /message, GET /stream/{id}, GET /conversations/{id}, POST /conversations/{id}/end, WS /ws/{id}.
The Analytics APIs (WI-176-178) shall provide 3 endpoints: GET /api/analytics/summary, GET /api/analytics/intents, GET /api/analytics/gaps.
Shopify GDPR mandatory webhooks (WI-35) shall be implemented: customers/data_request, customers/redact, shop/redact.
A whoami endpoint MUST be implemented to return the authenticated user's identity and role
SystemPromptBuilder (#70) MUST be implemented as a Tier 2 work item
The pipeline budget MUST reflect a P50 of 7,000ms and a timeout at 8,000ms
Stream-then-validate (Option A) MUST be used for the pipeline response delivery pattern
The SSE identity stream MUST send a synthetic 'stage' event before 'token' events so the widget creates a message bubble for identity preprocessor responses
All non-administrators MUST only have permission to interact with the inbox.
When a customer attempts to chat, the system MUST make every attempt to authenticate and identify them — customer identification and authentication for every conversation is critical (P0)
Anonymous chat sessions MUST be discouraged because they undermine the value of Persistent Customer Memory
The warning for anonymous customers MUST NOT be skipped — customers who choose not to identify themselves MUST know what benefits they lose and MUST confirm after the warning is displayed
The warning is deliberate friction to discourage anonymity in an e-commerce context
Customer identification MUST NOT be deprioritized or deferred unless the owner explicitly agrees — this is a permanent priority mandate

The customer identity flow MUST follow this sequence: (1) AI greeting and Quick Actions presented when chat opens, (2) customer inputs message or selects Quick Action, (3) AI checks Shopify auth and whether email is known — if NOT, AI requests it, (4) customer chooses anonymous or provides email, (5) if email provided, OTP sent, customer enters code, authenticated
Add-on modules MUST each indicate which entitlements are required for activation (Starter, Pro, Pro+, Enterprise)
Add-on modules not available for the current tier MUST NOT be subscribable; the Subscribe control MUST show the minimum required entitlement
Completing an Upgrade MUST change the available subscribable add-on modules to match the new entitlement level
The 'Priority support' add-on MUST be deferred to post-launch and removed from the admin UI
The 'White-label package' add-on MUST be deferred to post-launch and removed from the admin UI
The tier badge on the Billing page shall reflect the tenant's actual subscription entitlement (not a hardcoded value).
Upsell presentation shall follow the pattern of 'Conversation Packs' — clear value proposition with purchase flow.
Metering documentation shall map directly to billing practices, since billing will be regularly investigated by merchants who dispute charges.
Affiliate revenue from third-party integrations is deferred until after launch.
If the entitlement is set to 'cancelled', the tenancy MUST continue to operate normally until the next scheduled expiration check
While a tenancy is Expired, the widget key MUST be disabled (not allowed)
While a tenancy is Expired, the activation control in the merchant admin UI MUST display the message 'Expired'
If a merchant administrator interacts with the activation control while in 'Expired' state, a pop-up MUST offer the option to purchase a Starter, Professional or Enterprise license
The 'Subscribe' button on the Billing page MUST function correctly
Pricing MUST have a very low entry point with a 12-24 element matrix of compelling scaling tiers and high-value feature options.
Pricing MUST target 80% gross margin over cost of goods sold at list prices.
Pricing MUST be 50% lower cost than the nearest technologically comparable competitor at a comparable level of capability and quality.
Pricing MUST include usage-based overage charges with defined thresholds for AI model use per tier.
Transparent usage pricing MUST be implemented as a competitive advantage (transparent usage reduces customer dissatisfaction and improves retention).
Pricing MUST start with an itemized structure (Option C), allowing future bundles and promotions rather than starting with bundled pricing that would require list price adjustments.
The ConversationMeter feature MUST be approved and implemented as designed.
Dedicated model training add-on MUST be deferred to post-launch and used as a trigger for fresh marketing activity ('continuous innovation' proof point)
Rewardful affiliate integration MUST be set up for the affiliate program
The Stripe product catalog MUST include subscription tiers (Starter, Professional, Enterprise) with monthly and annual pricing plus metered overage using Stripe Billing Meters
The Stripe product catalog MUST include conversation packs (1K, 5K, 20K) and add-on modules (Multi-Language, Advanced Analytics, Priority Support)
Brand voice MUST be a mandatory configuration field
Language support at launch MUST be English only, with Spanish and French planned next
All configuration page changes are drafts until activated using the 'Activate' control
All transient keys, values, URLs, and variables that change between builds or tenant environments MUST NOT be hardcoded; they MUST be retrieved from tenant config database or env.local
Configuration status 'Not configured' MUST NOT exist; valid states are 'Activated' and 'Pending' only
The 'Roll back' option MUST NOT be available if the environment has never been Activated
Implement CQ-7: merchant quality configuration allowing merchants to set quality thresholds and preferences.
Refactoring R1 (ConfigProcessor extraction) shall be approved and implemented.

The configuration activation model shall use a three-state lifecycle: Active (live config) -> Pending (unsaved draft changes) -> Activate (with validation gates). Discard reverts to Active. Roll back restores previous version.

The tenant config interface shall include: (1) a distinct config processor separate from any GUI, (2) A/B testing of customer populations, (3) a simplified decision-making UI, (4) step-by-step initial setup workflow, (5) pervasive tooltips and documentation links for every config decision, (6) recent data adjacent to config inputs to inform decisions.
The Activate option MUST only appear when there are unapplied (saved but not yet activated) changes
The Restore option MUST reverse all saved-but-not-activated changes back to the prior known activated state
Restore MUST revert all three configurable pages together as a single atomic action
Option B approved: configuration snapshots MUST be stored immediately; history UI is deferred to a future release
WI #64 (configuration validation layer) approved for implementation
When the tenant environment is initialized, it MUST NOT be in an 'Active' state
System activation MUST NOT report failure while the system actually transitions to 'Active' state — the result reported to the user MUST match the actual state
The 'Inactive' state change MUST be triggered when the admin saves configuration changes that remove mandatory fields — must work as a client-server round trip, not just client-side
The missing-field validation on Activate MUST NOT use a two-step dialog flow; it MUST show all missing fields immediately in a single display
Escalation notification emails MUST support multiple categories: Sales, Support, Service, Account, Technical Assistance, General Inquiry
A/B testing MUST use a foundation model to design, run, and evaluate tests to reduce burden on the merchant

A/B test prompts MUST contain current configuration, and prompt responses MUST contain variations on that configuration (different persona descriptions, escalation thresholds, response length, formality level, enabled features)
The A/B test workflow MUST include an "I'm feeling lucky" button that prompts an AI model for a variation on the current configuration with user commentary/preferences and data
The merchant's role in A/B testing MUST be narrowly scoped to editorial review and test-approval; AI handles test design, execution, and evaluation
Test Mode MUST route X% of real storefront visitors to the AI agent configured with the test workflow while other visitors get the store's existing experience.
The Test Mode option MUST NOT be available until all necessary inputs have been selected for the first time and a complete configuration is in a 'Production' state.
The option to disable the escalation/report-issue message MUST be available for Professional and Enterprise tiers but MUST remain mandatory for the Starter (entry-level) tier.

A 'Clean re-install' or 'Reset' configuration MUST exist that includes example records and values sufficient for a merchant administrator to study as best-practice examples (e.g., every prompt or quick action has an easy-to-grasp example)
The widget_greeting_message field MUST NOT be set to empty string during deactivation — empty string overrides platform defaults, while None allows defaults to apply
Escalated conversations MUST show up when using the escalation filter
Implement CQ-1: conversation quality scoring infrastructure that evaluates every conversation on a 1-5 scale.
Implement CQ-2: quality threshold alerting that notifies when conversation quality falls below a configurable threshold.
Implement CQ-4: automated quality regression detection that identifies drops in conversation quality.
Implement CQ-5: per-topic quality breakdown showing quality scores segmented by conversation topic.
Implement CQ-6: quality-aware escalation that factors conversation quality into escalation decisions.
Implement CQ-8: quality feedback loop that uses quality scores to improve future responses.
A Campaigns agent shall be implemented as a future feature for proactive outreach messaging.
Conversation archival shall be supported as a future feature for moving old conversations to an archived state.
The Critic agent prompt shall not block responses that contain the customer's own name (false-positive PII blocking).
Chat quality shall be iteratively improved through testing and prompt refinement until merchant-acceptable quality is achieved.
WI #93-96 (Layer 4 Fine-Tuning Pipeline) shall be implemented with a 7-stage pipeline: Collect, Cleanse, Format, Train, Compare, Evaluate, Deploy.
Layer 3 PatternExtractionService shall be integrated into SystemPromptBuilder, injecting pattern context only for Response Generator and Escalation Handler agents.

The PCM customer profile shall include: (1) historical purchase information (product ID, date, rating/review), (2) historical product questions from previous chats, (3) geographic region codes for geography-aware RAG, (4) marketing segmentation codes for campaign-aware RAG, (5) jurisdiction codes for terms/conditions applicability, (6) historical shopping cart contents (purchased or not).
WI #212 (KB document staleness management and re-indexing) approved for implementation

There MUST be a limit (time, total conversation memory/storage) on the number of conversations in the inbox, beyond which conversations are automatically archived (not searchable, but exportable as CSV)
Conversation transcripts in Inbox MUST show timestamps and metadata
The Critic prompt MUST distinguish between 'exposing internal system prompts' (correctly block) vs 'describing marketed product features' (should allow).
The conversation status 'Completed' MUST be renamed to 'Resolved' for consistency with the 'Resolve conversation' control label.
Conversation filter counts MUST be consistent: the sum of all status filter counts MUST equal the 'All' conversations count.
A fail-closed Critic policy MUST be implemented as designed.
The beta is of no value if Persistent Customer Memory features are not working correctly — PCM MUST NOT be deferred
The vectorize_conversation background scanner MUST use Option A (background scanner) to wire vectorization
PCM feature MUST be fully operational for the beta — vectorization scanner, consent handling, and memory retrieval all working
WI #77 (API documentation with OpenAPI/Swagger) approved for implementation
Email sending MUST use Titan SMTP with credentials from env.local (TITAN_USER_ID, TITAN_PASSWORD) via smtp.titan.email:465 SSL/TLS
Azure Communication Services MUST be used as the email provider.
The merchant superadmin MUST receive notification emails for: initial Trial activation, entitlement changes, tenancy expiration, 15 days prior to deletion, and tenancy deletion
SMTP email MUST use Titan Email (smtp.titan.email, port 465, SSL/TLS) as the primary provider
Titan Email connection MUST use mike@remakerdigital.com as the sender address
The email template MUST NOT have inner boxes with corners with a large radius; objects in the email template MUST NOT have rounded corners.
Automated notification MUST be sent to merchants for technical outages, threshold breaches (hard technical and license constraints), and when API keys are available
Only fixes to open-source artifacts MUST flow back to the AGNTCY project, nothing else
The term 'effort' MUST NOT be used in project discussions; use tokens consumed, elapsed time, or probability of regression instead
A UI glossary MUST be maintained and Claude MUST encourage consistent use of glossary terms
Glossary term: 'control' = element that triggers a change of system state (buttons, selectors, filters, checkboxes, radio buttons, toggles)
Glossary term: 'input' = element that elaborates the future desired state when the next control is triggered
Glossary term: 'card' = a logically interdependent group of elements bounded by a bordered box, may contain controls, information, and inputs
Glossary term: 'picker' = a color or date selection element
Glossary term: 'configuration manager' = the top-level CONFIGURATION control group (Activate/Discard/Roll back)
Glossary term: 'configuration pages' = the individual pages within the configuration manager
Glossary term: 'error banner' = inline error notification; 'error pop-up' = overlay error notification
Glossary term: 'badge' = a small label indicator (e.g., status badge, role badge)
Release 1.0 MUST be full-featured and competitive, comparable to Tidio in features and usability
The 1.0 user experience MUST be polished and refined, comparable or superior in styling and usability to Tidio
The owner personality note MUST record: Mike values honest feedback — never exaggerate the quality or impact of his input
Documentation links MUST use HTTPS, not HTTP
All references to the old GitHub repo URL MUST be updated to the new URL: https://github.com/Remaker-Digital/agent-red-customer-engagement

The strategic direction shall prioritize conversation quality over cost reduction, with four priorities in order: (1) quality of individual conversations, (2) consistency across conversations, (3) operational visibility, (4) cost efficiency.
The AGNTCY multi-agent platform shall be adopted as the underlying agent orchestration framework.
The agent pipeline shall use a 6-agent containerized architecture: Intent Classifier, Knowledge Retrieval, Response Generator, Critic/Supervisor, Escalation Handler, Analytics Collector.
Tidio shall be the primary functional reference for feature parity and UX patterns.
The product shall be renamed from 'Customer Engagement' to 'Customer Experience'.
The brand primary color shall be #C41E2A (later updated to #ff3621).
Refactoring R7 (import path normalization) shall be implemented as a quick win.
Refactoring R8 (dead code removal) shall be implemented as a quick win.
Refactoring R9a (type annotation completion) shall be implemented at P0 priority.
Refactoring R10 (AGNTCY Phase 3 alignment) shall be implemented as part of AGNTCY Phase 3.
iubenda shall be adopted as the legal compliance provider (Option 4: iubenda for privacy/terms, LegalZoom retained for other legal purposes).
Language support priorities: Spanish (Mexico) and French (Canada) near-term, Portuguese (Brazil) and UK English medium-term.

A work item shall be created to investigate: (A) customer satisfaction impact of reducing P50 response latency below 1,500ms, and (B) cost of options to reduce P50 to minimum including custom AI models and fine-tuning, producing a cost/benefit chart.
All newly identified work items from the coverage report MUST be enumerated and added to the backlog
Claude is delegated priority choice for WI #209-225 (RAG infrastructure) implementation order
Effort estimates MUST NOT be presented to the owner. No metric other than elapsed real-world time is acceptable if estimates are ever requested.
The primary focus MUST be on quality: correctness, completeness, and absence of defects — not effort.
All non-superseded blocked capabilities (including C8 and C9) MUST be included in the release scope.
Launch timing is the owner's decision. Claude MUST disregard any concern for launch deadlines and not use launch as a reasoning factor.
The 1.0 release MUST be a full GA release for general availability — not a beta or soft launch.
The Agent Red website MUST initially be hosted within the remakerdigital.com domain (under an 'Applications' category). A standalone Agent Red website on Azure is deferred to the 1.1 release.
The GitHub wiki MUST use diagrams, charts, tables, mermaid and other graphical representations wherever possible. The wiki will be used by design and UX consultants to understand the system.
After testing is complete, implement new UI/UX features to close the gap with competitors.
All remaining 1.0 release work items that are not blocked by owner input MUST proceed in parallel while the owner works on blocking items.
Memory issues (uncontrolled memory loss, context drift) MUST be treated as CRITICAL priority and diagnosed immediately when detected.
No effort estimates MUST be provided — focus on quality (correctness, completeness, absence of defects)
The term "wordmark" refers specifically to "Customer Experience", not the text in primary-logo-no-wordmark.svg

The documentation website (agentredcx.com) 'Welcome' word MUST be removed from the main page so that the logo is at the top of the pane with 180px of bottom margin, pushing 'Agent Red Customer Experience, A product of Remaker Digital' text further down.
The SVG comment in primary-logo-light.svg MUST read 'Light Background'.
The customer memory personalization approach MUST be referred to as 'Persistent Customer Memory'.
The four-layer personalization stack (Persistent Customer Memory) MUST be highlighted in marketing materials, documentation, website and anywhere business value pillars are enumerated.
MCP support MUST be implemented as a prerequisite for credible claims of contemporary 'Agentic' implementation.
Claude MUST actively offer feedback on the owner's inputs during working sessions, including guidance on clarity, consistency, and effectiveness.
Technical work (implementation, testing, new capabilities) MUST have elevated priority over creative/content work (marketing, cosmetic).
Agent Red implementation roadmap priorities MUST be: (1) Improve customer experience, (2) Improve merchant administrator experience, (3) Implement with high quality to reduce CAC and churn.
The AGNTCY open-source project and Agent Red MUST be strictly isolated — Agent Red MUST interact with AGNTCY exclusively via GitHub, not share local artifacts or Docker containers
Work items MUST be presented one at a time with information and choices, pausing for owner input before proceeding — this is the preferred iterative working style
Logo concept 3 (The Beacon/AR monogram) MUST be used as the brand logo
Documentation quality framework MUST include Vale config, markdownlint config, CI workflow, and feature inventory
Mermaid diagrams MUST be consistently used wherever they could illustrate relationships between system elements, APIs, or input/output standards

Platform analysis MUST pay special attention to addressable market size, partner diversity (positive indicator), and partner program benefits — joining an ecosystem with established competitors that can be matched on technology/features/quality but beaten dramatically on price
Remaining work items MUST be grouped by overlapping knowledge for efficient implementation

When evaluating options, MUST prioritize: (1) implementation quality (efficiency, robustness, reliability, usability), (2) desirability (competitive, differentiating, superior usability), (3) downstream confidence (documentation, maintenance, testing)
Avoid generalizations such as 'simpler' or 'harder' — state specifically what is gained or lost: which protocols, failure modes, components, test coverage implications
The logo corners MUST be 90 degrees, NOT rounded
Category images representing the 10 most popular Shopify merchant categories MUST be created showing Agent Red as a helpful assistant in physical-world scenarios

Remaining 1.0 release work items MUST include: (1) UI validation, (2) Shopify approval verification, (3) launch process, (4) PCM test data, (5) competitor comparison table, (6) tooltip alignment, (7) automated merchant notifications
Technical work MUST have elevated priority over creative/content work — technical implementation, testing, and new capabilities prioritized above creative assets and marketing
No effort estimates — focus on quality (correctness, completeness, absence of defects)
GitHub repository MUST be strictly associated with the project board and vice-versa
Quality framework MUST implement the Diataxis documentation framework with quality standards applied before writing guides
Honest feedback MUST be provided — never exaggerate quality or impact of owner's input
Shopify and Stripe partner program approval criteria and enhanced relationship benefits MUST be documented
The Diataxis documentation framework MUST be used with updated plan incorporating quality standards before writing content
KB changes MUST participate in the Save-then-Activate flow: KB creates/edits/deletes mark config as Pending, activation deploys KB state alongside other config, Discard reverts KB changes
There MUST be a way to restore (unarchive) an article that has been archived
RAG configuration MUST cover both merchant-provided documents/information and Persistent Customer Memory feature
The merchant superadmin MUST be able to watch the ingestion happen if possible — it should be a visible, foreground process
The RRF (Reciprocal Rank Fusion) scores MUST be normalized to a 0-1 range so they can exceed the MIN_RELEVANCE_SCORE threshold
The KB seed script MUST be idempotent — running it multiple times MUST NOT create duplicate entries
A parallel production-capable dev/test environment MUST be created for future releases
A detailed step-by-step process for non-disruptive upgrade MUST include provisioning scripts and a regression test package
The NATS connection issue shall be resolved using Option B (the selected fix approach).
Session wrap-up shall include updating knowledge base, cleaning artifacts, committing to GitHub, updating the GitHub project and wiki, and providing a next-session initialization prompt.
The agentredcx.com documentation site shall be updated with details of new features and UI changes after each deployment.
The next session initialization prompt shall load all UI-related knowledge but take no action until the owner begins manual functional testing.
A comprehensive item-by-item review of all outstanding work items shall be conducted, producing a Master-Plan-Review status report capturing all decisions.
tenant_id shall be included on all Application Insights telemetry for per-tenant observability.
A correlation ID chain shall be maintained across all 6 agents in the conversation pipeline for end-to-end request tracing.

Backup handling shall include encryption, key management, and a known process for offloading to warm or cold storage. Data shall be retained for years to support future model training and predictive pattern identification.
A priority ordering of all outstanding work items shall be maintained and followed when proposing tasks for completion.
WI #29 (secrets management via Azure Key Vault) approved for implementation
WI #47 (KEDA autoscaling) approved for implementation
WI #48 (Terraform infrastructure-as-code) approved for implementation
WI #52 (horizontal pod autoscaling) approved for implementation
WI #55 (monitoring and alerting) approved for implementation
WI #58 (CI/CD pipeline) approved for implementation
WI #59 (blue-green deployment) approved for implementation
A security assessment of the local development environment MUST be performed
All issues reported by beta customers MUST be corrected immediately and fixes MUST be pushed to the beta (production) environment with highest priority.
Session wrap-up MUST include: knowledge base updates, cleanup of unnecessary artifacts, commit and push to GitHub, GitHub project/wiki updates, and a next-session initialization prompt.
Service recovery documentation MUST retain all information needed to recreate the environment from scratch, including noting any key invalidation that would affect customers.
The system MUST be recoverable from a catastrophic failure (like Azure account termination) with no major impact to customers.
The upgrade verification procedure MUST be parameterized with a TARGET_ENVIRONMENT variable so it works for both staging and production
The system MUST have a documented procedure for non-disruptive upgrade of the current deployment environment.
The system MUST have a documented process for creating a branch for new release development, testing and deployment.
The Knowledge Database graphical UI MUST only provide a read interface (filter, order, sort, search) to the owner; the owner MUST NOT have the ability to make any changes.

SCHEDULE.md MUST contain groupings of sequential prompts arranged so that groups are executed in sequence, with the first group always chosen and deleted once all prompts in that group have been communicated.
Claude MUST have permission to update SCHEDULE.md to add future events based on predictions about when certain operations will be required.
New build/test MUST be performed in a parallel (staging) environment; beta production MUST remain stable and undisturbed during development.
stripe.exe MUST be added to .gitignore to prevent accidental commit of the binary.
CLAUDE.md historical information MUST be decomposed: rename to CLAUDE_ARCHIVE.md and create a pruned CLAUDE.md with a reference to the archive.
Verbose logging MUST be enabled on every part of the system during pre-production work

The release plan MUST follow: (1) complete testing with 100% pass, (2) freeze release and branch, (3) provision beta tenants, (4) gather feedback, (5) enhance next release on parallel production, (6) non-disruptive upgrade
Tag-and-branch-forward (Model A) MUST be used as the branching model — no long-lived development branches
Periodic audit sessions MUST occur with a clean context for self-care and inventory/inspection of assets and plans
When beta feedback fixes are made, all changes MUST be applied in the next release version — beta prime runs a pinned release image
Non-disruptive upgrade MUST be proven on staging before applying to beta prime production
A Shopify purchase event shall trigger automated tenant provisioning without manual intervention.
Harrison Corp and Kidsely shall be provisioned as beta test tenants.
Cosmos DB partition key shall be tenant_id for all tenant-scoped collections.
The Trial tier shall be implemented with: 50 conversation limit, 5 rpm rate limit, 2 concurrent sessions, 14-day history retention, Layer 1 PCM only, 0 overage rate, 14-day duration.
A demo/tryout environment MUST be provided for customers before subscription purchase
The seed script MUST initialize tenants with config_state='draft', NOT 'active'.
When creating a new tenant, the environment MUST be seeded with default values via a seed process. The seed script serves as both initialization and functional evaluation.
New tenant provisioning and health check MUST be fully automated with 100% success rate and no manual involvement (P0 blocker)
Provisioning MUST be migrated from in-memory dicts to Cosmos DB TenantRepository (WI-C1)
auto_provision_superadmin() MUST be wired into handle_checkout_completed webhook handler (WI-C2)
The seed_tenant.py script MUST correctly generate widget keys that pass auth (dual-write hash to TenantDocument)
A fresh deployment MUST be in its initialized state with zero conversations and not in 'Active' state.
Initialization MUST create a tenancy that contains no configuration or information from prior to initialization.
Tenant provisioning MUST never fail silently and MUST recover from faults
SPA console MUST provide a means to set manually-created tenancies to EXPIRE at a future date — without this, manually created tenancies exist indefinitely
Beta customer provisioning MUST use the Starter tier with no expiry date during the beta period
The Status checkbox MUST be removed from team management.
End-to-end tests MUST be run on a freshly initialized environment, not one with stale data
100% UI test coverage means every possible input and value for every possible state has been checked
100% UI test coverage MUST include every button pushed, every selection selected, every state for a given set of inputs saved
100% UI test coverage MUST include testing every type of deliberate misconfiguration
UI testing MUST include the first-login case on a freshly initialized environment as superadmin
UI testing MUST include the 'I lost my API keys' path
Refactoring R4 (test fixture consolidation) shall be implemented at P1 priority.
All remaining P1 pre-launch tests MUST be completed without pausing for owner input between tests
Adversarial testing MUST be performed in the production environment
Complete testing of everything already implemented is the top priority — higher than implementing new features.
End-to-end automated tests MUST verify the setup is complete and correct AND baseline performance on a freshly initialized system.
A master procedure for end-to-end testing MUST exist that specifies sub-procedures, execution order, and is iteratively corrected until a complete run has no errors or omissions

For the interactive UI review, each element MUST be evaluated for: (1) necessity given current plan, (2) correct text and presentation type, (3) correct behavior on interaction, (4) revealed controls also evaluated, (5) image visibility, (6) correct colors for Light/Dark mode, (7) mandatory inputs marked with red asterisk
Plan-gated elements MUST be tested per entitlement tier — a full set of tests may be needed for each plan (Trial, Starter, Professional, Enterprise)
Repeatable Procedures for automated testing of UI via Chrome MUST be created, with Chrome access used to the maximum extent to automate all UI validation and readiness/correctness testing

For each UI element validation: (1) ensure it is displaying, (2) it displays correct information, (3) inputs can be manipulated to produce correct response, (4) inputs are populated with valid information, (5) changing input results in correct state change, (6) control activation results in correct state change, (7) input validation is correct with warning/failure/explanation, (8) each disposition is tested with appropriate messages

When performing interactive UI tests: if inputs are required, provide inputs; if the system handles different inputs differently, try all types; if an input blocks another test, provide the blocking input first; tests MAY be multi-step sub-procedures to maximize coverage
A mock tenant script MUST be able to set the state of an environment to approximate a fresh tenant for testing purposes
It is acceptable to take the test environment offline (Deactivation) for testing purposes

Entitlement enforcement MUST be testable via a private control that validates the UI enforces configuration based on entitlements data, with changes reflected in real-time when a customer upgrades or purchases an optional feature

The test tenant MUST have 19 conversations: 6 merchant pages with 2 quick actions each (12 conversations with escalation triggers routed to each target), 2 unassigned for manual escalation testing, and 1 un-escalated for archive control testing
Test conversations MUST go through the AI pipeline (not bypass it) to allow testing of SPA console tracing, monitoring, and stats — no data should take non-production paths through the system
Once the Simulated Customer Tenant is created, its known state MUST make it easier to test non-disruptive upgrade, entitlement changes, and license entitlement enforcement

It is acceptable to pause re-testing of code paths believed unaffected by changes and focus on testing only changed paths, with a full end-to-end test run once all new implementation work items are finished

Testing Repeatable Procedures MUST include tests that verify: (1) test PII injected into a conversation is correctly scrubbed, (2) consent prompt appears, (3) a GDPR deletion request successfully deletes all system information for the identified customer.
The UI review process MUST be granular and iterative: start with Dashboard in Dark Mode, then switch to Light Mode, then proceed page-by-page.
All testing MUST be modularized to allow cooling cycles and/or use multiple cores in bursts to prevent heat-related system instability
A functional test harness is mandatory — untestable implementation is unacceptable
If critical-path errors are encountered, testing MUST immediately cease and new work items MUST be planned to resolve defects before resuming from the beginning
Every run of end-to-end tests MUST re-seed the tenant for a clean state in every respect
The SPA provisioning test MUST serve as the pre-test leading directly to the critical-path test
If the owner finds an issue during manual testing, a regression test MUST be added to prevent it from recurring — this MUST be general guidance loaded for each session
No pre-existing failures MUST be accepted for the 1.0 GA release gate — known failing tests must be fixed or removed with justification before execution begins
The chat widget MUST support a Dark Mode option
The chat UI MUST be responsive and web-based, supporting both mobile and desktop customer users
The chat UI MUST be click-draggable to other regions of the browser window
The widget iframe shall use height 100% to fill its container.
A mobile-optimized widget variant shall be provided as a future feature for small-screen devices.
The Hugo chat UI shall be used as a functional reference for the widget chat experience.
The vestigial auto-open behavior in the widget shall be removed.
The widget tenant config endpoint shall pass appearance fields (colors, fonts, dimensions) as data attributes on the widget container element.
Closing the chat widget with the 'X' button MUST NOT leave the widget in the wrong state
A future work item MUST be created for an 'AI Greeting' option toggle that, when enabled, lets the AI generate greeting messages for new chat sessions

The Widget 'Pre-chat form' MUST have a tooltip informing that the account associated with the customer at login may not be a reliable identifier, that if discerning multiple users sharing one Shopify account matters they should ask users to identify themselves, and that information collected is not verified and not related to security
Widget error messages (e.g., "Failed to start conversation") MUST use a medium grey background color, NOT the brand primary color (#ff3621)
The widget preview MUST render and support open/close interaction
Widget chat responses MUST include streaming (tokens appear progressively)
The widget MUST support clickable textual 'Quick Action' buttons in the chat messaging area that trigger pre-made prompts seeded with page-specific information.
Web crawlers MUST be blocked from triggering billable conversations OR crawler conversations MUST be classified as non-billable.
